channel.title: Project Overview on neon•Golem
channel.description: Recent content in Project Overview on neon•Golem

item.title: Hello, neon•Volt.
item.description:
neon.Volt? What?

It’s a portable smart power supply that runs off of USB-C, utilises the rev 2 power delivery spec and is configurable over UART, but also comes with physical buttons, so you can use it without a computer. At the time of me writing this, it’s still in the system design phase, but I do have a preliminary spec for it and that goes as follows: 100W total power, 98.5% efficient at 20V input, 10mV adjustment steps, controllable via PC command line (so it’s scriptable) or via the knobs and keys on the device. I will not be able to guarantee that efficiency and the converter will probably not be that precise, especially with cable drop at higher currents. I can’t foresee everything that will go wrong with it, but I’ll do my best to make it work as best I can. There’s been feature creep. What started out with me adding hardware to meet the system specification ended up being “hey, if i’m doing this anyway, I might as well do more of the same” and that’s how I arrived at a full 16 bit user addressable terminal block, a user accessible ADC and DAC pins, a dedicated 3V3 rail and an adjustable output of 1 to 48 volts, a color 320x240 screen and enough flash memory to comfortably run Doom on this thing.
